best_for: founder essays. Equally good for tech
or business decks that want a Sunday-supplement warmth instead of corporate polish.
avoid_when: Decks that need visual heat or punch — the warm-paper palette and Cormorant serif are intentionally quiet.

typography.style: high-contrast garalde serif headlines paired with a clean humanist sans body

visual_dna.typography_role: high-contrast garalde serif headlines paired with a clean humanist sans body

best_for: and weighty-graphic: museum and cultural-institution decks
manifestos. A great choice anytime — including across tech and business — when you want the deck to feel like a field manual rather than a slide deck.
avoid_when: Contexts that demand digital-native polish or playful pop — the stencil-cut display and earth-tone palette commit to a deliberate analog feel.

typography.style: ultra-heavy display + stencil + tall condensed + clean body; reads like an archaeological field manual

visual_dna.typography_role: ultra-heavy display + stencil + tall condensed + clean body; reads like an archaeological field manual

best_for: Anything that should feel electric and design-led: studio credentials
sneaker brand work. Also a striking unexpected choice for tech
or business decks where the speaker wants the deck to *be* a brand statement.
avoid_when: Contexts that should feel quiet or institutional — the black-and-electric-yellow palette is the loudest in the library.

typography.style: broadcast-grotesk display + technical mono captions; ultra-high-contrast typographic system

visual_dna.typography_role: broadcast-grotesk display + technical mono captions; ultra-high-contrast typographic system

best_for: founder reflections. Equally strong for any deck — including tech
or creator work — that wants a calm
considered atmosphere instead of energetic visuals.
avoid_when: Contexts that need visual heat or pop — the navy + warm-yellow Cormorant aesthetic is intentionally low-tempo.

typography.style: transitional serif as the structural display face, paired with clean DM Sans body and Courier Prime mono for attributions and labels; bilingual EN/CN support

css_variables.sz_lead:
var(--sz-body)">
  • One claim, fully argued
  • One accent, used once
  • Body cut to the bone

visual_dna.typography_role: transitional serif as the structural display face, paired with clean DM Sans body and Courier Prime mono for attributions and labels; bilingual EN/CN support
